message:
chore(beads): update issue tracker state

Sync beads issue JSONL with latest bead status changes from concurrent
agent work. Updates closed/in-progress timestamps and acceptance
criteria for ftui migration and analytics beads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -476,7 +476,7 @@
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.12","title":"Task: Semantic search model manager UI on ftui (availability, download/install, progress, policy)","description":"## Goal
Port the semantic model setup experience (currently integrated into Ratatui TUI):
- show semantic availability state
- allow download/install of model files (when policy allows)
- show progress (bytes, steps)
- handle failures gracefully

## Constraints
- No automatic downloads in headless mode.
- Respect existing env/policy behavior (`CASS_SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER`, headless constraints described in README).

## Notes
- Reuse existing implementation modules:
  - `src/search/model_download.rs`
  - `src/search/model_manager.rs`

## Tests
- Unit tests for semantic availability state machine.
- Snapshot tests for download modal/progress rendering.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can set up semantic search entirely from within the TUI.
- Failures provide actionable guidance (missing files list, expected paths).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:24.423004893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:48.310585199Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","semantic","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.12","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:48.007721141Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.12","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:24.423004893Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.12","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:48.310533342Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","title":"Task: Mouse support on ftui (hit testing, click-to-select, scroll panes, pill interactions)","description":"## Goal
Preserve and improve mouse support:
- click to select results
- scroll results/detail panes
- click pills/breadcrumbs to edit/remove

## Implementation Notes
- Use ftui frame hit grid registration.
- All actions must remain keyboard accessible (mouse is optional).

## Tests
- Simulation tests: inject mouse events and ensure correct Msg/actions.
- Snapshot tests for hit regions are not directly visible, but we can test:
  - selected state after click

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mouse capture is enabled only when supported and cleaned up on exit.
- Mouse behavior does not interfere with terminal scrollback in inline mode.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:33.757395033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:49.240899604Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","ftui","mouse","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:48.671831919Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:33.757395033Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:49.240827469Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:48.955386040Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.14","title":"Task: Source provenance display + source filter UX on ftui (local/remote, per-host menus)","description":"## Goal
Port and polish multi-source UX:
- show source indicators in results (e.g., `[laptop]`)
- support cycling source filter (all → local → remote → all)
- support per-source selection menu

## Notes
- `README.md` documents `F11`/`Shift+F11` source filtering.
- Ensure provenance fields (`source_id`, `source_kind`, `workspace_original`) are visible in detail view/raw view.

## Tests
- Unit tests for source filter state transitions.
- Snapshot tests where mixed local+remote results show correct badges.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Source filtering works consistently with CLI flags (`cass search --source ...`).
- UI makes it obvious where a conversation originated.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:44.633710226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:49.787804524Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","sources","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:44.633710226Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:49.524316108Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:49.787715127Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","title":"Task: Theme + style system implementation on ftui (presets, opt-outs, contrast/a11y patterns)","description":"## Goal
Implement the real ftui theme system used by all widgets:
- theme presets (dark/light/high-contrast + any existing cass presets)
- role-aware styling (user/assistant/tool/system)
- zebra stripes, gutters, highlights
- opt-out env vars (NO_COLOR, CASS_NO_COLOR, CASS_NO_ICONS, CASS_NO_GRADIENT, etc.)
- a11y patterns (when `CASS_A11Y=1`) so color is not sole carrier of meaning

## Notes
- This is the implementation counterpart of the spec work in `coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.2`.
- Prefer `ftui_style::{Theme, ThemeBuilder, StyleSheet, ColorProfile}`.

## Tests
- Unit tests for contrast computations and preset invariants.
- Snapshot tests across presets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All core panes use the same token source (no ad-hoc per-widget colors).
- Truecolor terminals get premium theme; 8/16-color terminals get safe fallback.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:58.811941817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:39.370357792Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","ftui","style","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:38.553479922Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:38.962901148Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:39.370250843Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:58.811941817Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","title":"Task: Theme + style system implementation on ftui (presets, opt-outs, contrast/a11y patterns)","description":"## Goal
Implement the real ftui theme system used by all widgets:
- theme presets (dark/light/high-contrast + any existing cass presets)
- role-aware styling (user/assistant/tool/system)
- zebra stripes, gutters, highlights
- opt-out env vars (NO_COLOR, CASS_NO_COLOR, CASS_NO_ICONS, CASS_NO_GRADIENT, etc.)
- a11y patterns (when `CASS_A11Y=1`) so color is not sole carrier of meaning

## Notes
- This is the implementation counterpart of the spec work in `coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.2`.
- Prefer `ftui_style::{Theme, ThemeBuilder, StyleSheet, ColorProfile}`.

## Tests
- Unit tests for contrast computations and preset invariants.
- Snapshot tests across presets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All core panes use the same token source (no ad-hoc per-widget colors).
- Truecolor terminals get premium theme; 8/16-color terminals get safe fallback.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:58.811941817Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:30:10.247332542Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","ftui","style","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:38.553479922Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:38.962901148Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:39.370250843Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.15","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:30:58.811941817Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.16","title":"Task: FocusGraph navigation system on ftui (directional traversal, focus trapping, focus ring)","description":"## Why
The current TUI uses a simple FocusRegion enum (Results/Detail) with manual tracking and focus_flash_until timer. ftui provides a proper FocusGraph for navigation that handles the complexity of modals, nested widgets, and directional movement.

## What
Replace FocusRegion with ftui_widgets::focus::FocusGraph. Define focus nodes:
1. **SearchBar** (text input focus)
2. **FilterPills** (each pill focusable for edit/delete)
3. **ResultsList** (item selection focus)
4. **DetailPane** (scroll focus)
5. **CommandPalette** (modal focus trap)
6. **ExportModal** (modal focus trap)
7. **HelpOverlay** (modal focus trap)

FocusGraph handles:
- Directional navigation (Up from Results → SearchBar, Down from SearchBar → Results)
- Tab/Shift+Tab cycling through all nodes
- FocusTrap ensures modal focus doesn't leak to background widgets
- Visible focus ring indicator (colored border or glow effect) on focused widget

## Implementation
- Use frame.register_hit() for each focusable region
- FocusTrap activated when any modal is open
- Focus ring rendered as themed border highlight (uses agent accent colors)
- Replaces manual focus_region tracking and focus_flash_until timer

## Tests
- Unit: FocusGraph navigation sequence tests (Tab cycles, directional moves)
- Unit: Modal focus trapping prevents escape
- Snapshot: Focus ring renders correctly in each theme
- E2E: Full keyboard navigation flow (search → results → detail → modal → back)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing focus transitions preserved (no regression)
- Tab order matches logical UI flow
- Modals trap focus; Esc releases
- Focus ring visible in all 6+ themes with WCAG contrast
- CASS_DISABLE_ANIMATIONS=1 disables focus transition animations

## Depends On
- 2noh9.3.1 (layout foundation must exist first)
- 2noh9.2.3 (Program skeleton must be running)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:18.698317676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:29.107507028Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:29.107442839Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:18.698317676Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:26.431410925Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.17","title":"Task: Clipboard integration via OSC52 on ftui (copy paths, content, code blocks; works over SSH)","description":"## Why
The current TUI has basic clipboard via crossterm Paste events, but lacks reliable cross-terminal clipboard write support. OSC52 is the escape sequence that allows terminal apps to read/write the system clipboard without external dependencies — critical for multi-machine search (works over SSH).

## What
Leverage ftui-extras/clipboard for OSC52 clipboard integration:
1. **Copy selected result path+line** to clipboard (y or Ctrl+Y)
2. **Copy detail pane content** to clipboard (Ctrl+C in detail view without selection)
3. **Copy code block** from detail view (keybinding on specific code block)
4. **Copy search query** to clipboard
5. **Paste from clipboard** into search bar (Ctrl+V — unified API replacing raw crossterm Paste)

## Implementation
- ftui-extras clipboard module handles terminal capability detection (OSC52 support probe)
- Graceful fallback: if OSC52 not supported, try xclip/xsel/pbcopy
- Visual feedback: brief toast notification on successful copy ("Copied path to clipboard")
- Multi-select copy: Ctrl+Y copies all selected item paths separated by newlines

## Tests
- Unit: OSC52 escape sequence generation is correct
- Unit: Fallback chain works (OSC52 → xclip → noop with warning)
- Integration: Copy→paste roundtrip with ProgramSimulator
- E2E: Copy result path, verify clipboard content

## Acceptance Criteria
- Works over SSH sessions (OSC52 passthrough)
- Falls back gracefully on terminals without OSC52
- Toast confirms copy operation
- All existing y/Ctrl+Y behavior preserved

## Depends On
- 2noh9.2.3 (Program skeleton must be running)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:34.903656751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:33.479806754Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.17","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:33.479689506Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.17","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:34.903656751Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","title":"Task: Search bar UX on ftui (text input, history, suggestions, debounce, match modes)","description":"## Goal
Recreate and improve the search bar experience:
- search-as-you-type with debounce (still sub-60ms perceived latency)
- Up/Down history navigation + de-dup
- Ctrl key edits (clear, delete token)
- Enter forces immediate search
- Match modes (standard/prefix) and wildcard fallback indicator
- Search modes (lexical/semantic/hybrid) toggle

## Implementation Notes
- Use `ftui-widgets::input` or `textarea` for editable text.
- State transitions should be reducer-style and unit-testable.
- Search execution should be cancelable/coalesced (avoid piling requests during typing).

## Tests
- Unit tests for query editing + history behavior.
- Deterministic simulation tests using `ftui_runtime::ProgramSimulator` for:
  - typing "auth" and seeing results update after debounce
  - Up/Down history nav

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior matches parity matrix for all documented keybindings.
- No input lag or dropped keystrokes under heavy rendering.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:28:10.964810150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:41.442234617Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","search","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:41.039710098Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:41.442165218Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:40.617283649Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:28:10.964810150Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -1406,9 +1406,9 @@
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.2","title":"Task: Live ingest analytics (message_metrics + batched rollups)","description":"## Goal\nCompute token/tool/plan metrics once at ingest time and maintain hourly/daily rollups with minimal indexing overhead.\n\n## Implementation Plan\n1. Add hour_id/day_id helpers (hour_id based on UTC hours since a fixed epoch; day_id consistent with existing daily_stats helper).\n2. During batched message inserts:\n   - compute message_metrics fields (created_at_ms, role, dims, content chars + est tokens)\n   - extract API usage tokens via existing extract_tokens_for_agent() helpers\n   - compute tool_call_count + has_tool_calls (start with existing extraction coverage)\n   - compute has_plan (cheap heuristic)\n3. Insert message_metrics rows in the same transaction as messages.\n4. Accumulate rollup deltas in-memory keyed by (bucket_id, agent_slug, workspace_id, source_id).\n5. Flush rollups via multi-value INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE (no per-message upserts).\n\n## Hard Constraints\n- No JSON parsing in hot paths that already have parsed structures (reuse connector-normalized fields).\n- Rollup flush is batched once per transaction/chunk, not per message.\n- All writes must be transactionally consistent (messages, message_metrics, rollups update together).\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- New indexed messages always create exactly 1 message_metrics row.\n- usage_hourly and usage_daily match summed message_metrics for a synthetic fixture (exact equality).\n- Indexing throughput regression is negligible (measure before/after on fixture corpus; record results).\n\n## Testing & Logging\n- Unit: hour_id/day_id conversion edge cases (timezone, boundaries).\n- Integration: insert synthetic conversations and assert fact+rollups correctness.\n- Logging: emit a single per-batch debug log with counts + timing (no per-message logs).\n","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:47:29.872630196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:25:54.542852835Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:25:54.542826807Z","close_reason":"Implemented live ingest analytics pipeline: MessageMetricsEntry, AnalyticsRollupAggregator, has_plan_heuristic, batch insert/flush functions. Wired into insert_conversations_batched. 2 integration tests pass. All 2379 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","indexing","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:33Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","title":"Epic: Analytics CLI + Robot Contract (cass analytics …)","description":"Epic for the complete robot-first analytics CLI surface (`cass analytics ...`).

Deliver a stable, machine-operable command family that exposes analytics health, token time series, tool/model/cost breakdowns, rebuild workflows, and validation checks with deterministic JSON contracts. The CLI must share semantics with the analytics query library and FrankenTUI dashboards to prevent drift.

This epic also includes robot-docs contract publishing and test coverage that locks command behavior, schema stability, and failure diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:47:56.690284560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:30:43.541374393Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cli","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:46Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:46Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:46Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":193,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Analytics CLI Contract (Robot-First)

This comment is the canonical CLI/JSON contract for the analytics surface. The intent is: **agents can use analytics without reading code**.

## Command Tree (v1)
- `cass analytics status --json`
- `cass analytics tokens --json`
- `cass analytics models --json`
- `cass analytics cost --json`
- `cass analytics rebuild --json`
- `cass analytics validate --json`

Notes:
- Subcommand names and flag names must remain stable once published.
- stdout = JSON data only; stderr = diagnostics.

## Common Flags (all analytics subcommands)
- Time window:
  - `--since <time>` / `--until <time>` (ISO, relative like -7d, keywords today/yesterday)
  - convenience presets: `--days N`, `--today`, `--week`
- Filters:
  - `--agent <slug>` (repeatable or comma list)
  - `--workspace <path>` (repeatable or comma list)
  - `--source <local|remote|all|source_id>`
  - `--role <user|assistant|tool|system|all>` (advanced; may require deep/slow path)

## Output: `analytics status`
Purpose: a fast, high-signal snapshot for both humans and automation.

Suggested JSON shape:
```json
{
  "db_path": "...",
  "tables": {
    "message_metrics": {"rows": 123, "min_ts": 0, "max_ts": 0},
    "usage_hourly": {"rows": 0, "last_updated": 0},
    "usage_daily": {"rows": 0, "last_updated": 0},
    "token_usage": {"rows": 0, "min_ts": 0, "max_ts": 0},
    "token_daily_stats": {"rows": 0, "last_updated": 0}
  },
  "coverage": {
    "api_usage_message_pct": 0.0,
    "model_name_message_pct": 0.0,
    "notes": ["..."]
  },
  "drift": {
    "possible": false,
    "signals": ["..."],
    "recommended_action": "none|rebuild_all|rebuild_message_metrics|rebuild_token_usage"
  },
  "_meta": {"elapsed_ms": 0}
}
```

## Output: `analytics tokens`
Purpose: time-series usage.

Required capabilities:
- group-by: hour/day/week/month (UTC)
- dims: agent/workspace/source
- metrics:
  - api tokens: total/input/output/cache_read/cache_creation/thinking
  - content tokens: content_tokens_est_total
  - counts: message_count, user_message_count, assistant_message_count, tool_call_count, plan_message_count
  - coverage: api_coverage_pct
- derived:
  - api tokens per assistant msg
  - content tokens per user msg
  - tool calls per 1k tokens
  - planning share (once v2 plan attribution exists)

Response should be stable and ordered:
```json
{
  "group_by": "day",
  "filters": {"since": "...", "until": "...", "agent": ["claude_code"], "workspace": [], "source": "all"},
  "buckets": [
    {"key": "2026-02-01", "metrics": {"api_tokens_total": 0, "content_tokens_est_total": 0, "message_count": 0, "api_coverage_pct": 0.0}, "derived": {"api_tokens_per_assistant_msg": null}}
  ],
  "_meta": {"elapsed_ms": 0, "path": "rollup|slow_path", "warning": null}
}
```

If we must scan `message_metrics` (slow path), `_meta.path` must say so.

## Output: `analytics models`
- top models by API tokens and/or content-est tokens
- model coverage (percentage of messages with model_name)
- unknown bucket must be explicit

## Output: `analytics cost`
- estimated USD totals using `model_pricing`
- coverage diagnostics:
  - unknown pricing models
  - estimate-only vs api-usage
- must never present USD with false precision

## Output: `analytics rebuild`
- must be explicit about what is rebuilt:
  - Track A: message_metrics + usage_* rollups
  - Track B: token_usage + token_daily_stats + conversation summary columns

If we support `--all` / `--track a|b`, document it here.

## Output: `analytics validate`
- invariants results with actionable errors
- include sample bucket IDs and expected vs actual for failures

## Implementation Constraint
All query semantics (bucket boundaries, week/month rules, derived metrics) must live in `crate::analytics` (z9fse.12) so CLI and FrankenTUI dashboards cannot drift.
","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:20:45Z"},{"id":194,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Update (2026-02-06): Add `cass analytics tools` to the CLI Contract

The command tree in the prior contract comment is missing the per-tool surface. We are explicitly adding it so other agents can reason about tool usage without scraping TUI screens.

## Command Tree (v1, updated)
- `cass analytics status --json`
- `cass analytics tokens --json`
- `cass analytics tools --json`
- `cass analytics models --json`
- `cass analytics cost --json`
- `cass analytics rebuild --json`
- `cass analytics validate --json`

## Output: `analytics tools`
Purpose: per-tool breakdown over a time window, with trends.

Minimum JSON shape (exact field names can be finalized in z9fse.12, but keep structure stable once shipped):
```json
{
  "group_by": "day",
  "filters": {"since": "...", "until": "...", "agent": [], "workspace": [], "source": "all"},
  "tools": [
    {
      "tool_name": "Read",
      "invocation_count": 0,
      "message_count_with_tool": 0,
      "metrics": {"api_tokens_total": 0, "content_tokens_est_total": 0, "api_coverage_pct": 0.0},
      "derived": {"avg_api_tokens_per_invocation": null}
    }
  ],
  "other": {"invocation_count": 0, "metrics": {"api_tokens_total": 0}},
  "_meta": {"elapsed_ms": 0, "path": "rollup", "warning": null}
}
```

Performance rule: if tool rollups exist (z9fse.6), this must be O(#buckets + #tools) and must not scan full message tables.","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:37:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1","title":"Task: CLI scaffolding for analytics command tree (clap + help + docs hook)","description":"## Objective
Build the production-grade analytics CLI skeleton so all analytics commands share one stable robot contract and one centralized argument model.

This task is the hard foundation for every downstream analytics command (`status`, `tokens`, `tools`, `models`, `cost`, `rebuild`, `validate`). If this shape is wrong, every later task drifts.

## Why This Matters
- Gives other agents a deterministic command surface they can depend on.
- Prevents duplicated flag parsing and inconsistent JSON behavior.
- Makes analytics discoverable via `--help` and `robot-docs` before deep features land.

## Scope
1. Add `cass analytics` command tree in clap wiring.
2. Register subcommands with stable names:
   - `status`
   - `tokens`
   - `tools`
   - `models`
   - `cost`
   - `rebuild`
   - `validate`
3. Define shared analytics flags and parsing model:
   - time range (`--since`, `--until`, `--days`)
   - dimensional filters (`--agent`, `--workspace`, `--source`)
   - bucketing (`--group-by hour|day|week|month`) where relevant
   - output mode (`--json` and robot-safe behavior)
4. Add dispatch stubs for each subcommand (even if implementation is deferred).
5. Wire placeholder hooks for `cass robot-docs analytics` topics so docs work can attach to a stable route.

## Contract Rules
- `stdout` must be data-only JSON in robot mode.
- Human diagnostics must go to `stderr`.
- Exit-code semantics must follow existing cass conventions.
- Do not introduce ad-hoc per-command parsing; all analytics commands must reuse shared parser structs/helpers.

## Test + Logging Requirements
- Unit tests for clap parsing:
  - command aliases and required args
  - repeated filter args
  - invalid enum values produce actionable errors
- E2E CLI tests (non-interactive) for:
  - `cass analytics --help`
  - each subcommand help
  - `--json` mode parseability and deterministic top-level schema envelope
- Failure logs must include command, exit code, stdout, stderr, and elapsed milliseconds.

## Out of Scope
- Computing analytics data itself (owned by child command tasks and query library work).","acceptance_criteria":"1. `cass analytics --help` lists all required subcommands and shared analytics flags with consistent wording.
2. All analytics subcommands parse common filters identically and reject malformed values with clear errors.
3. Robot mode behavior is uniform (`stdout` JSON, diagnostics on `stderr`, stable exit-code handling).
4. CI includes unit + e2e tests for parsing/help output, with detailed failure logs.
5. Downstream command tasks can implement data logic without touching clap tree plumbing.","notes":"Design intent: keep this task narrow but high leverage. It should enable fast parallel implementation of command-specific analytics tasks while preventing contract drift.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:12:19.273361340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:09:05.328483863Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:57:09.806789511Z","close_reason":"Completed: CLI scaffolding landed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cli","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:12:19.273361340Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":195,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Started implementation: added analytics CLI scaffolding contract tests in tests/cli_dispatch_coverage.rs (help coverage, shared flags, parser mapping, uniform JSON envelope, invalid enum error guidance). Validation run is currently blocked by unrelated compile error in src/storage/sqlite.rs (moved model_family ownership); requested fix from reservation holder SilentRobin via Agent Mail thread coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1.","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:56:11Z"},{"id":196,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: extended tests in tests/cli_dispatch_coverage.rs for analytics command tree (subcommand help, shared flag parsing, envelope consistency, models variant mapping, invalid group-by error quality). Validation currently blocked by unrelated workspace compile failure in src/ui/app.rs (missing ftui symbols Layout/Paragraph). Coordinating via Agent Mail thread coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1.","created_at":"2026-02-06T18:09:05Z"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.2","title":"Task: CLI analytics status (row counts, freshness, coverage, drift warnings)","description":"## Objective
Implement `cass analytics status --json` as the canonical health/quality endpoint for analytics data.

This command should answer, in one shot, whether analytics is trustworthy right now and what action to take if it is not.

## Scope
1. Report table-level state for both analytics tracks:
   - Track A: `message_metrics`, `usage_hourly`, `usage_daily`
   - Track B: `token_usage`, `token_daily_stats`, plus conversation summary coverage signals
2. For each table include high-signal metadata:
   - row counts
   - min/max timestamp coverage where applicable
   - last update timestamp
3. Report coverage diagnostics (not just totals):
   - API-token coverage %
   - model-name coverage %
   - estimate-only share
   - unknown/empty-model counts
4. Detect drift and coherence failures between tracks:
   - one track fresh while other is stale
   - missing rollups while source facts exist
   - rebuild mode mismatch symptoms
5. Emit recommended remediation action in output:
   - `none`
   - `rebuild_all`
   - `rebuild_track_a`
   - `rebuild_track_b`

## Output Contract
- Deterministic, machine-parseable JSON schema with explicit sections:
  - `tables`
  - `coverage`
  - `drift`
  - `_meta`
- No hidden heuristics; include concrete drift signals list so operators understand *why* a warning fired.

## UX Requirements
- Fast path (<~200ms on medium DBs, barring cold I/O).
- Human-readable warnings on stderr in non-json mode; JSON stays clean in stdout.

## Test + Logging Requirements
- Unit tests:
  - drift heuristic evaluation
  - coverage math edge cases (zero rows, partial rows)
- Integration tests with fixture DB states:
  - healthy
  - stale Track A
  - stale Track B
  - contradictory row-count/timestamp states
- E2E tests for command output stability:
  - parse JSON and verify required keys exist
  - verify recommended action under seeded drift states
- Detailed logs on test failure must include:
  - fixture profile
  - computed drift signals
  - emitted recommendation","acceptance_criteria":"1. `cass analytics status --json` returns a stable schema containing table stats, coverage metrics, drift signals, and recommended action.
2. Drift detection correctly distinguishes healthy vs stale/misaligned analytics tracks on fixture databases.
3. Coverage values are mathematically correct and robust for sparse/empty datasets.
4. Command behavior is robot-safe (`stdout` JSON only, diagnostics on `stderr`).
5. Unit/integration/e2e tests exist with high-signal logs that make failures immediately actionable.","notes":"This command is the first line of defense for analytics trustworthiness. Other analytics commands should be gated or at least annotated when status reports significant drift.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:12:32.203470918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:51:15.796879314Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cli","contract","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:12:32.203470918Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:12:32.203470918Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.3","title":"Task: CLI analytics tokens (hour/day/week/month + dims + derived averages)","description":"Implement `cass analytics tokens --json`: time-series token/usage analytics.

## Required Capabilities
- `--group-by hour|day|week|month` (UTC buckets; weeks are ISO-8601 weeks, Mon-start)
- Filters (repeatable / comma-list):
  - `--agent <slug>`
  - `--workspace <path>` (resolved to workspace_id)
  - `--source <local|remote|all|source_id>`

## Metrics (v1)
Must be representable from rollups for O(#buckets) queries:
- Counts: `message_count`, `user_message_count`, `assistant_message_count`, `tool_call_count`, `plan_message_count`
- Content estimate: `content_tokens_est_total`, `content_tokens_est_user`, `content_tokens_est_assistant`
- API tokens: `api_tokens_total`, plus components (`api_input_tokens_total`, `api_output_tokens_total`, `api_cache_*`, `api_thinking_tokens_total`)
- Coverage: `api_coverage_message_count` and derived `api_coverage_pct`

## Derived Metrics (v1)
- `api_tokens_per_assistant_msg` (null if assistant_message_count=0)
- `content_tokens_per_user_msg` (null if user_message_count=0)
- `tool_calls_per_1k_tokens` (define denominator explicitly; include both:
  - per 1k API tokens (when api_tokens_total>0)
  - per 1k content-est tokens)
- Planning:
  - **v1**: `plan_message_pct = plan_message_count / message_count`
  - **v2** (z9fse.14): `plan_token_share` (token-attributed), either via new rollups or an explicit slow-path.

## Performance Contract
- Prefer rollups:
  - hour: `usage_hourly`
  - day/week/month: `usage_daily` (aggregate for week/month)
- If a requested slice cannot be served by rollups, return a structured slow-path indicator in `_meta` (and optionally require an explicit `--deep`).

## Output Contract
- Stable ordering by bucket key.
- stdout = JSON only; stderr = diagnostics.
- `_meta` must include `{ elapsed_ms, path: "rollup"|"slow", warning?: string }`.

## Testing & Logging
- Integration (z9fse.8): fixture spanning multiple days/hours; totals match across group-bys.
- Unit: ISO week/month boundary correctness + divide-by-zero derived metrics.
- Logging: query spans include group_by, filters, rows read, elapsed_ms.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:12:49.109509308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:24:39.344253729Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:24:39.344230195Z","close_reason":"Completed: analytics tokens CLI with rollup queries, time bucketing (hour/day/week/month), dimensional filters, derived metrics, structured JSON output. 9 new tests + 4 existing pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cli","contract","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:12:49.109509308Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:12:49.109509308Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.4","title":"Task: CLI analytics rebuild (expose rebuild/backfill with progress + JSON output)","description":"Expose rebuild/backfill via CLI: `cass analytics rebuild --json`.

## Requirement: Be Explicit About Tracks
Analytics rebuild must be explicit about what is rebuilt:
- **Track A**: `message_metrics` + `usage_hourly` + `usage_daily`
- **Track B**: `token_usage` + `token_daily_stats` + conversation token summary columns

## CLI Surface
- `cass analytics rebuild --track a|b|all --json`
  - `--track a` should remain the default until Track B rebuild exists.
  - `--track all` runs A then B.
  - Consider `--all` as an alias for `--track all` for ergonomics.

## Output Contract (Robot-First)
- stdout: JSON only
- stderr: diagnostics/progress
- JSON includes:
  - requested track
  - per-track result: rows inserted/affected, elapsed_ms, rows_per_sec
  - overall elapsed_ms

## Integration
- Wire to the rebuild implementation from **z9fse.13** (track selection lives in storage, not CLI).
- Integrate with `cass analytics status` drift signals (status after rebuild should show drift=false).

## Acceptance Criteria
- On fixture DB, `--track a` rebuild succeeds and returns stable JSON output.
- After z9fse.13 lands, `--track all` rebuilds both tracks and status shows no drift.

## Testing & Logging
- E2E (extend z9fse.8): run rebuild and assert:
  - exit 0
  - JSON includes per-track sections + elapsed
  - stderr includes progress diagnostics
- Logging: structured spans (track=a|b, rows, elapsed_ms); no per-row spam.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:04.959241773Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:25:19.914432117Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cli","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:04.959241773Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:04.959241773Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:04.959241773Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- JSON includes:
  - requested track

  - overall elapsed_ms

- Integrate with `cass analytics status` drift signals (status after rebuild should show drift=false).

- Logging: structured spans (track=a|b, rows, elapsed_ms); no per-row spam.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:04.959241773Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:31:56.050757581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cli","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:04.959241773Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:04.959241773Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:04.959241773Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.5","title":"Task: CLI analytics validate (rollup invariants + drift detection)","description":"## Objective
Implement `cass analytics validate --json` as the correctness gate for analytics math and rollup integrity.

`status` tells us if data *looks* healthy; `validate` must prove key invariants actually hold.

## Scope
1. Implement fast invariant checks (default mode):
   - non-negative counters/tokens
   - required key columns present and non-null for aggregate keys
   - rollup rows exist for periods with source facts
2. Implement aggregate consistency checks:
   - sampled bucket sums in rollups equal recomputed sums from fact tables within tolerance
   - per-dimension totals reconcile with global totals
3. Implement cross-track drift checks:
   - Track A totals vs Track B totals are directionally consistent where overlap exists
   - identify stale/partial recomputation patterns
4. Provide two validation depths:
   - `--mode fast` (default): high-signal checks under ~1s on fixture DB
   - `--mode deep`: broader recomputation windows for CI/nightly confidence

## Output Contract
Return deterministic JSON with:
- `summary` (pass/fail + severity)
- `checks` array (id, status, measured, expected, tolerance, message)
- `drift_signals`
- `recommendations` (specific actions, not generic)
- `_meta` (elapsed_ms, mode, sample strategy)

## Performance + Safety
- Must be read-only and safe on live DB.
- Must fail closed: if a check cannot execute, report explicit `error` status rather than silently passing.

## Test + Logging Requirements
- Unit tests per check function and tolerance logic.
- Integration tests with intentionally corrupted fixture datasets:
  - off-by-one rollup
  - negative token rows
  - stale track mismatch
- E2E tests:
  - validate success case returns `pass`
  - seeded failure case returns `fail` with actionable checks
- Logging on failure must include:
  - failed check id
  - measured vs expected values
  - bucket/dimension keys used in comparison","acceptance_criteria":"1. `cass analytics validate --json` emits stable JSON listing individual checks and an overall pass/fail summary.
2. Default mode runs quickly on fixture DB while still catching intentionally seeded corruption.
3. Deep mode increases confidence by expanding recomputation scope without changing check semantics.
4. Cross-track drift and rollup mismatch errors are clearly diagnosable from output alone.
5. Unit/integration/e2e coverage includes detailed logs for every failing check.","notes":"This command will be used as a preflight gate before trusting dashboards and before performance benchmarking runs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:23.122319809Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:28:01.827598307Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cli","performance","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:23.122319809Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:23.122319809Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:23.122319809Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.6","title":"Task: CLI analytics models (top models + coverage stats)","description":"## Objective
Implement `cass analytics models --json` to expose model-level usage behavior with full coverage diagnostics.

This command should make it trivial to answer: which models drive token usage, where model attribution is missing, and how trustworthy model-level analytics are.

## Scope
1. Provide model rankings by multiple metrics:
   - API token totals
   - content-token estimates
   - message counts
   - optional cost (when pricing coverage exists)
2. Support time grouping and filters:
   - hour/day/week/month
   - agent/workspace/source filters
3. Include coverage and attribution quality:
   - % messages with model_name
   - unknown/unmapped model buckets
   - provider/family normalization quality indicators
4. Include both per-model and rollup summaries:
   - top-N table
   - long-tail aggregation into `other`
   - global totals for sanity checks

## Output Contract
Deterministic JSON with sections:
- `filters`
- `group_by`
- `models` (ordered, stable tie-breaking)
- `coverage`
- `totals`
- `_meta`

Model rows should expose:
- canonical model key
- optional display name
- metrics block (tokens/counts)
- derived block (shares, per-message averages)

## Implementation Considerations
- Reuse analytics query library (`z9fse.12`) so CLI and FrankenTUI consume identical semantics.
- Ensure unknown models are first-class rows, not silently dropped.
- Keep bucket boundaries UTC and consistent with tokens command.

  - model normalization and family grouping

  - unknown bucket behavior
- Integration tests:

  - sparse model attribution
  - filtered windows
- E2E tests:
  - JSON schema stability
  - deterministic top-N output from fixture DB

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.7","title":"Task: CLI analytics cost (USD estimates + pricing coverage)","description":"## Objective
Implement `cass analytics cost --json` for transparent USD estimation with explicit uncertainty and pricing coverage diagnostics.

Cost numbers are high-stakes; the command must never imply precision that the data does not support.

## Scope
1. Compute estimated cost from model pricing and token usage:
   - input/output/cache/thinking token components when available
   - explicit fallback rules when only partial token classes are present
2. Provide breakdowns by:
   - time bucket (day/week/month)
   - model family / model
   - agent/workspace/source filters
3. Emit pricing coverage quality:
   - % token volume matched to known pricing
   - list of unknown/unpriced models and affected token share
   - estimate-only warnings when API usage is sparse
4. Provide derived economics metrics:
   - cost per 1k API tokens
   - cost per assistant message
   - period-over-period delta

## Output Contract
Deterministic JSON sections:
- `filters`
- `pricing_coverage`
- `buckets`
- `breakdowns`
- `totals`
- `_meta`

Every monetary value should include explicit units (`usd`) and nullable semantics where computation is impossible.

## Precision / Safety Rules
- Use decimal-safe handling appropriate for currency.
- Avoid hidden rounding; round only for presentation layers while retaining raw numeric fields.
- Expose caveats in output when cost is partial.

## Test + Logging Requirements
- Unit tests:
  - pricing rule matching
  - cost math across token classes
  - rounding and nullability behavior
- Integration tests:
  - mixed priced/unpriced model datasets
  - sparse API token coverage
  - period breakdown correctness
- E2E tests:
  - stable JSON schema
  - known fixture yields expected totals and warnings
- Logging on failure must include pricing match trace and unmatched-model diagnostics.","acceptance_criteria":"1. `cass analytics cost --json` reports USD estimates plus explicit pricing coverage diagnostics.
2. Unknown pricing and partial token coverage are surfaced clearly and never silently ignored.
3. Monetary calculations are reproducible and robust to sparse/partial inputs.
4. Time and dimension breakdowns are consistent with analytics query-library semantics.
5. Unit/integration/e2e tests with rich logs validate pricing mapping, math, and warning behavior.","notes":"Treat cost output as an analytics estimate with explicit confidence, not a billing ledger.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:48.442243175Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:36:07.287036785Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cli","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:48.442243175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:48.442243175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:13:48.442243175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
